hypothesis: In REINVENT4 singlet fission optimization, using a PID controller to adapt NaviDiv similarity-, fragment-, and n-gram-penalty weights to maintain mean pairwise Morgan Tanimoto similarity at 0.12±0.01 will produce ≥30% more unique Bemis-Murcko scaffolds with predicted ∆ESF in [−0.10, 0.00] eV than fixed combined-constraint settings, while keeping the average molecular score within 5% of the fixed-baseline.
